title: Digging into the costs of urban soil rehabilitation
data_sources: Institut Paris Région / CDC Biodiversité renaturation manual (Castaing et al., 2022)

data_scope.geography: France (French regulatory and labor cost context)
data_scope.respondent_count: not stated in abstract; full details in Supplementary Sections 1–6

core_conclusion: In France, median urban soil rehabilitation costs range from €50–310 m−2 for compacted, sealed, or built-up soils and exceed €800 m−2 for polluted soils. Rehabilitation involves up to ten sequential steps; preliminary diagnostics are cheapest while building deconstruction and pollution remediation are most expensive. These first-ever cost benchmarks support spatial prioritization of rehabilitation areas and the design of economic incentives, while also underscoring the value of preventing soil degradation.
why_it_matters: First quantitative cost baseline for urban soil rehabilitation; enables cost-benefit analyses for urban greening and climate adaptation; translates France's Zero Net Artificialization law into an economic liability framework; decomposes costs by step to reveal policy leverage points; open Zenodo dataset supports replication and European scaling.
why_nature: Fills a critical evidence gap in urban sustainability science; directly relevant to EU Soil Monitoring Law, Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, and French national soil policy; methodologically novel expert-elicitation approach; bridges ecology and economics in alignment with Nature Cities' interdisciplinary mission.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatially explicit cost mapping via GeoAI + MGWR: model rehabilitation cost as a spatially varying surface using remote sensing and land registry data
Flood-damage benefit-transfer model: pair per-m2 costs with inundation-avoided estimates to compute net-benefit ratios by city density zone
Restorative accessibility gap metric: integrate rehabilitation costs into 15-minute city / 2SFCA framework as a cost-weighted green-space supply variable
Explainable ML cost-prediction tool: SHAP attribution on the Zenodo survey dataset to quantify which cost drivers (soil type, pollution, engineering intensity) dominate
Equity-sensitive rehabilitation optimization: multi-objective facility-location solver that maximizes accessibility gains for deprived neighborhoods per euro of rehabilitation budget
